title: Basel III OPR25.1 — Operational risk event capture and loss-data aggregation
exclusions: Does NOT substitute for the full regulatory control set; this UC provides one of several evidence streams and must be combined with policy, procedure, and supplemental UCs to demonstrate the complete clause.
evidence: Scheduled search output persisted to the compliance summary index with regulation=Basel III, clause=OPR25.1, available as a saved search and exportable as a CSV evidence pack for the responsible officer.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Phase-C authored UC targeting Basel III OPR25.1 (Operational risk management). Uses continuous Splunk telemetry to aggregate operational risk loss events and near-misses; assurance level set to 'contributing' pending SME review per Phase E of the regulation-coverage plan.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that Basel III OPR25.1 (Operational risk management) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.50.24: Basel III OPR25.1 — Operational risk event capture and loss-data aggregation.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.50.24' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.
compliance.smeCaveat: Phase-C draft: SME review required before uplifting assurance from 'contributing' to 'partial' or 'full'. Confirm that the SPL captures the specific regulator expectation and that the telemetry exists in the deployment.

dataSources: Operational risk event logs, incident management systems (ServiceNow/Jira), financial loss ledger, near-miss reporting feeds.
spl:
index=risk_events sourcetype IN (oprisk_events, incident_mgmt) earliest=-30d
| eval loss_amount=coalesce(loss_amount, 0)
| eval risk_category=coalesce(risk_category, "unclassified")
| stats count AS event_count sum(loss_amount) AS total_loss dc(business_unit) AS affected_units values(risk_category) AS categories BY business_line, event_type
| eval severity=case(total_loss>=1000000, "critical", total_loss>=100000, "high", total_loss>=10000, "medium", true(), "low")
| where event_count>0
| sort -total_loss
| table business_line, event_type, event_count, total_loss, severity, affected_units, categories
description: Aggregates operational risk events, losses, and near-misses across business lines per OPR25.1 requirements for operational risk identification, assessment, monitoring, and reporting.
value: OPR25.1 mandates banks to identify, assess, monitor, and control/mitigate operational risk. Continuous loss-data aggregation provides the evidence base for the operational risk management framework.
implementation: (1) Ingest operational risk events from GRC/incident management platforms; (2) normalise loss_amount and risk_category fields; (3) schedule daily; (4) route critical-severity events to risk committee dashboard.
visualization: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.
knownFalsePositives: Tuning prerequisites: source field mappings must be in place before the UC will produce reliable metrics; early runs will commonly flag unmapped systems until onboarding is completed.
grandmaExplanation: OPR25.1 mandates banks to identify and track operational risk — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Operational risk event logs, incident management systems (ServiceNow/Jira), financial loss ledger, near-miss reporting feeds.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest operational risk events from GRC/incident management platforms; (2) normalise loss_amount and risk_category fields; (3) schedule daily; (4) route critical-severity events to risk committee dashboard.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=risk_events sourcetype IN (oprisk_events, incident_mgmt) earliest=-30d
| eval loss_amount=coalesce(loss_amount, 0)
| eval risk_category=coalesce(risk_category, "unclassified")
| stats count AS event_count sum(loss_amount) AS total_loss dc(business_unit) AS affected_units values(risk_category) AS categories BY business_line, event_type
| eval severity=case(total_loss>=1000000, "critical", total_loss>=100000, "high", total_loss>=10000, "medium", true(), "low")
| where event_count>0
| sort -total_loss
| table business_line, event_type, event_count, total_loss, severity, affected_units, categories
```

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.
